display.purpose: Course and lesson page titles only.

slide-display.purpose: Projected slide headings that must scale with viewport size while using Thai-safe line height and top padding so vowels and tone marks stay unclipped.

body.purpose: Long-form Thai explanations with comfortable line height.

code.purpose: Code blocks and inline code examples.

shadows.name: none-at-rest
shadows.purpose: Default depth model for panels, code blocks, diagrams, tables, and slide frames.

motion.name: state-transition
motion.purpose: Default hover and focus transition for links, buttons, and cards.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Use for the primary start action on course pages.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px; border: 0; border-radius: var(--radius-sm, 6px); background: var(--accent, #007acc); color: var(--text-strong, #ffffff); padding: 8px 16px; font-family: "IBM Plex Sans Thai", "Segoe UI", Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 600; line-height: 20px; cursor: pointer; transition: background 150ms ease, color 150ms ease; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: var(--accent-strong, #3794ff); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--accent-strong, #3794ff); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Secondary Button
components.description: Use for secondary navigation such as Extension Map or Document.
components.css: .ds-btn-secondary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px; border: 1px solid var(--border, #3c3c3c); border-radius: var(--radius-sm, 6px); background: var(--panel, #252526); color: var(--text-strong, #ffffff); padding: 8px 16px; font-family: "IBM Plex Sans Thai", "Segoe UI", Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 600; line-height: 20px; cursor: pointer; transition: border-color 150ms ease, background 150ms ease, color 150ms ease; } .ds-btn-secondary:hover { border-color: var(--accent-strong, #3794ff); background: var(--panel-soft, #2d2d30); } .ds-btn-secondary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--accent-strong, #3794ff); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Active Lesson Link
components.description: Sidebar lesson row that marks the current hour.
components.html: Hour 1Basic Website Workflow
components.css: .ds-nav-active { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 2px; border: 1px solid var(--accent, #007acc); border-radius: var(--radius-sm, 6px); background: var(--accent-soft, rgba(0,122,204,0.18)); color: var(--text-strong, #ffffff); padding: 8px 12px; font-family: "IBM Plex Sans Thai", "Segoe UI", Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif; font-size: 14px; line-height: 20px; text-decoration: none; transition: background 150ms ease, border-color 150ms ease; } .ds-nav-active:hover { border-color: var(--accent-strong, #3794ff); background: var(--selection, #264f78); } .ds-nav-hour { color: var(--muted, #858585); font-size: 12px; } .ds-nav-title { color: var(--text-strong, #ffffff); }

components.name: Course Card
components.description: Use for day summaries, lesson headers, and repeated course items.
components.html:
Day 1

Static Prototype and Git

Understand web workflow and create a static issue reporting prototype.
components.css: .ds-card { border: 1px solid var(--border, #3c3c3c); border-radius: var(--radius-sm, 6px); background: var(--panel, #252526); color: var(--text, #d4d4d4); padding: 20px; font-family: "IBM Plex Sans Thai", "Segoe UI", Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif; box-shadow: none; } .ds-card-kicker { margin: 0 0 4px; color: var(--accent-strong, #3794ff); font-size: 14px; font-weight: 600; line-height: 20px; } .ds-card-title { margin: 0; color: var(--text-strong, #ffffff); font-size: 24px; font-weight: 600; line-height: 32px; } .ds-card-body { margin: 8px 0 0; color: var(--muted-strong, #a6a6a6); font-size: 16px; line-height: 24px; }

components.name: Code Block
components.description: Code examples in MDX lesson prose.
components.css: .ds-code { width: 100%; overflow-x: auto; border: 1px solid var(--border, #3c3c3c); border-radius: var(--radius-md, 8px); background: var(--background, #1e1e1e); color: var(--text, #d4d4d4); padding: 16px; font-family: "SFMono-Regular", Consolas, "Liberation Mono", monospace; font-size: 16px; line-height: 28px; } .ds-code code { color: inherit; font: inherit; }

components.name: Slide Controls
components.description: Full-viewport slide mode controls with minimal chrome. Slide count belongs in the topbar beside the Document action. Dense MDX sections can use slide-only break markers so slides stay teachable without duplicating document content.
components.html: 1 / 17
components.css: .ds-slide-topbar { display: flex; justify-content: flex-end; align-items: center; gap: 12px; font-family: "IBM Plex Sans Thai", "Segoe UI", Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif; } .ds-slide-document, .ds-slide-counter { border: 1px solid var(--border, #3c3c3c); border-radius: var(--radius-md, 8px); background: var(--panel, #252526); color: var(--text, #d4d4d4); padding: 9px 11px; font-size: 14px; line-height: 20px; } .ds-slide-counter { min-width: 77px; text-align: center; font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums; } .ds-slide-controls { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 16px; border-top: 1px solid var(--border, #3c3c3c); background: linear-gradient(0deg, rgba(30,30,30,0.94), rgba(30,30,30,0.68) 72%, rgba(30,30,30,0)); padding: 14px 16px; backdrop-filter: blur(14px); font-family: "IBM Plex Sans Thai", "Segoe UI", Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif; } .ds-slide-icon { width: 42px; height: 42px; display: inline-grid; place-items: center; border: 1px solid var(--border, #3c3c3c); border-radius: var(--radius-md, 8px); background: var(--panel, #252526); color: var(--text, #d4d4d4); cursor: pointer; transition: border-color 150ms ease, background 150ms ease, color 150ms ease; } .ds-slide-icon:hover { border-color: var(--accent-strong, #3794ff); background: var(--panel-soft, #2d2d30); color: var(--text-strong, #ffffff); } .ds-slide-progress { height: 8px; flex: 1; overflow: hidden; border-radius: var(--radius-pill, 999px); background: var(--panel-soft, #2d2d30); } .ds-slide-progress span { display: block; width: 45%; height: 100%; border-radius: inherit; background: var(--accent-strong, #3794ff); }

narrative.overview: This system should feel like a practical teaching workspace: dark, quiet, code-adjacent, and ready for a 4-hour class session. The course-book and slide surfaces share one identity. The system explicitly rejects a marketing landing page, a decorative slide template, or a colorful showcase that competes with the lesson content.
keyCharacteristics: VS Code Dark Plus surfaces with restrained blue accent use.
One Thai-first sans family across UI, prose, diagrams, and slides.
Flat tonal layers, 1px borders, and 6-8px corners.
Document mode for reading and practice, slide mode for live teaching.
Controls stay predictable and familiar, not novel.

rules.name: The Accent-Earns-Its-Place Rule
rules.body: Blue is for action, current position, links, and diagrams. Do not use blue as decoration.

rules.name: The Dark-Layer Rule
rules.body: Depth comes from tonal steps: background, panel, raised panel, soft panel. Do not introduce unrelated tinted neutrals.

rules.name: The Thai-First Rule
rules.body: IBM Plex Sans Thai is the primary voice. Do not introduce a display face or decorative font for lesson UI.

rules.name: The Slide Exception Rule
rules.body: Fluid type belongs to slide headings only. Product UI labels, navigation, and document prose stay fixed and predictable.

rules.name: The No-Ghost-Card Rule
rules.body: Do not pair 1px borders with large soft shadows. Use borders and tonal surfaces instead.

rules.name: The Overlay-Only Rule
rules.body: Blur and translucent gradients are allowed on slide topbar and controls because they protect readability over the full-screen slide canvas. Do not use glass effects as decoration elsewhere.

dos: Do preserve the VS Code Dark Plus palette: Editor Background, Panel Charcoal, Text Gray, Strong White, and Workbench Blue.
Do use the accent only for action, current position, links, diagrams, progress, and semantic state.
Do keep long Thai prose readable with comfortable line height, clear heading scale, and predictable spacing.
Do make slide mode feel like the same content in a live-teaching format, not a separate deck with separate copy.
Do keep diagrams, tables, and code blocks visible, bordered, and easy to scan; use Teaching Flow Diagram for wide workflow diagrams that must remain readable in slide mode.
Do respect reduced motion and keep transitions limited to state feedback.
donts: Don't make the site feel like a marketing landing page.
Don't make the site feel like a decorative slide template.
Don't make the site feel like a colorful showcase that competes with the lesson content.
Don't hide core workflow diagrams, code, or checkpoints behind visual flourishes.
Don't introduce ornamental layouts, noisy effects, generic course-platform styling, or dense pages where learners cannot tell what to do next.
Don't use gradient text, oversized rounded cards, side-stripe borders, or large soft drop shadows.
